title: allophylus zeylanicus var. hispidus
intro: Allophylus zeylanicus var. hispidus, also known as the Sri Lankan allophyllus, is a plant species belonging to the Sapindaceae family, characterized by its thorny branches and yellow flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used in medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Allophylus zeylanicus var. hispidus include its potential to alleviate fever, relieve pain, and treat various gastrointestinal disorders. In terms of herbal preparations, the leaves and roots of the plant are often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat various health conditions.
